selection_rule: largest mean three-repetition reduction in absolute H+Z error after adding X; latest-consensus case excludes the early-case ticker when possible
rationale: Kalshi delivery ladder is very tight: Q1 deliveries are almost certainly between 350k and 360k, materially below Q4 2025 and only modestly above weak Q1 2025. That level supports a sequential decline in automotive revenue, while strong Energy backlog/deployments and Services should partially offset. The consensus of $22.691B appears achievable but somewhat high relative to the implied delivery volume and Q1 seasonality, so I expect a small revenue miss rather than a repeat of the large Q1 2025 shortfall.
without_x_rationale: Consensus implies about a 9% sequential revenue decline from Q4, which already embeds typical Q1 seasonality and some post-IRA-credit U.S. softness. I expect Q1 to be slightly better than that due to the larger exiting backlog cited on the Q4 call, ongoing APAC/EMEA strength, and continued energy-storage growth, partly offset by lower U.S. demand, S/X wind-down, mix pressure from lower-priced models, and the FSD shift toward subscription. Net result is a modest beat rather than a large upside surprise.
metric_label: how many tesla deliveries will there be this quarter

selection_rule: largest mean three-repetition reduction in absolute H+Z error after adding X; latest-consensus case excludes the early-case ticker when possible
rationale: Kalshi's pre-publication trading-volume ladder is centered just above $200B for Q1, below recent comparable quarters such as Q2/Q3 2025, pointing to softer spot-driven transaction revenue. Coinbase had $420M of transaction revenue through Feb. 10, but that likely reflected early-quarter volatility and should not be fully straight-line extrapolated. I estimate transaction revenue around $850M, subscription/services near the midpoint of guidance at roughly $590M, and other revenue around $60M, yielding total revenue near $1.50B, modestly below the $1.581B consensus.
without_x_rationale: Consensus appears to assume roughly straight-line extrapolation of the $420M transaction revenue generated through Feb. 10 plus the midpoint of Q1 subscription/services guidance. I expect a modest miss because management explicitly cautioned against extrapolating amid volatility, described a lower crypto-price and risk-off environment, and noted retail pullback despite active users buying dips. Using transaction revenue modestly below straight-line, subscription/services near the midpoint of the $550M-$630M guide, and normal other revenue yields total revenue around $1.51B.
